title: Cochlidium tepuiense
taxonomy: Cochlidium tepuiense is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Cochlidium, which is a group of tropical ferns native to South America. The family Polypodiaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns, with many species found worldwide. Cochlidium tepuiense is specifically native to the tepuis of Venezuela. This species is known for its unique characteristics and adaptations to its high-altitude environment.

intro: Cochlidium tepuiense is a species of epiphytic fern, a type of non-vascular plant that grows on other plants without deriving nutrients from them. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Cochlidium tepuiense include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
